message.reasoning_content: We need to fix the method chain: step-11 depends on step-14, but step-14 appears later (index 13, which is step-14). That violates the rule: depends_on must reference earlier steps. The dependency step-14 (escape_or_bound_assessment) should be earlier than step-11 (candidate_classification). Let's rearrange steps so that step-14 is before step-11. But the instruction says "NEVER renumber, reuse, or delete existing step ids". However, we can reorder the method_chain array such that step-14 appears before step-11. The ids remain the same, only their positions change. Also step-14 depends on step-06 and step-08. Step-08 depends on step-06 and step-07. So step-14 can be placed after step-08. Step-11 depends on step-10 and step-14. So step-11 must be after step-14. Currently step-10 (origin_assessment) depends on step-09, which is after step-08. We need step-14 after step-08, then step-10 after step-09, then step-11 after step-14 and step-10. However step-10 does not depend on step-14, so fine. Step-11 depends on step-10 and step-14. So we need step-14 placed after step-08 but before step-11. Also step-09 (orbit_integration) depends on step-06 and step-04, which are before step-08. So step-09 after step-08? Currently step-09 is after step-08. That's okay. We'll reorder the method_chain list to: step-01 ... step-08, then step-14, then step-09, then step-10, then step-11, then step-12, step-13, step-15, step-16. Ensure dependency ordering: step-14 depends on step-06, step-08 (both before). step-09 depends on step-06, step-04 (before). step-10 depends on step-09. step-11 depends on step-10, step-14. So step-11 must be after step-10 and step-14, which it will be. That fixes the error. Also check if any other step references step-14? No. So just reorder. The instruction "NEVER renumber, reuse, or delete existing step ids" allows reordering of the method_chain array as long as we don't change ids. So I'll produce a corrected JSON with the method_chain reordered such that step-14 comes before step-11. The order of steps in the array matters for validation; each step's depends_on must refer to earlier step ids in the list. So I'll rewrite the method_chain array accordingly. Also I need to ensure that all other steps still satisfy dependencies. I'll restructure: step-01 to step-08 as before. Then step-14 (escape_or_bound_assessment, depends_on step-06, step-08). Then step-09 (orbit_integration, depends_on step-06, step-04) — okay, those are before step-14. Then step-10 (origin_assessment, depends_on step-09). Then step-11 (candidate_classification, depends_on step-10, step-14). Then step-12 (quality_filter, depends_on step-02, step-06). Then step-13 (stellar_parameter_inference, depends_on step-02). Then step-15 (astrometric_calibration, depends_on step-02). Then step-16 (photometric_or_sed_modeling, depends_on step-02). All dependencies are satisfied. So I'll construct the JSON with this order. The rest of the JSON remains the same as previous scaffold. I'll output the full corrected JSON.
